content.problemStatement: Rectify the errors in the program to correctly print the last character of the string.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single string S.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the last character of the given string on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The last characters are 'f' from "CodeChef", 'd' from "HelloWorld", and 'a' from "a".
For "OpenAI" the last character is 'I'; for "ChatGPT" it is 'T'.
A single-character string returns that character itself.

solution.approach: The key insight is to simply access the last character of the input string. This can be done by finding the length of the string and then accessing the character at the index length - 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is a single character.
